Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 1345 upvotes, 186 comments
  2. U+237C ⍼ Is Azimuth 42 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. Exploring the ocean with Raspberry Pi–powered marine robots 28 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. Show HN: How I Topped the HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard on Two Gaming GPUs 246 upvotes, 79 comments
  5. Invoker Commands API 36 upvotes, 7 comments
  6. Cloudflare Crawl Endpoint 29 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. HyperCard discovery: Neuromancer, Count Zero, Mona Lisa Overdrive (2022) 83 upvotes, 23 comments
  8. Intel Demos Chip to Compute with Encrypted Data 211 upvotes, 81 comments
  9. Open Weights isn't Open Training 72 upvotes, 23 comments
  10. We are building data breach machines and nobody cares 86 upvotes, 29 comments
  11. Show HN: Joha – a free browser-based drawing playground with preset shape tools 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. I put my whole life into a single database 405 upvotes, 198 comments
  13. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 168 upvotes, 76 comments
  14. FFmpeg-over-IP – Connect to remote FFmpeg servers 90 upvotes, 39 comments
  15. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 501 upvotes, 290 comments
  16. Levels of Agentic Engineering 101 upvotes, 53 comments
  17. Universal vaccine against respiratory infections and allergens 32 upvotes, 13 comments
  18. Meta acquires Moltbook 371 upvotes, 246 comments
  19. Tell HN: Apple development certificate server seems down? 38 upvotes, 17 comments
  20. Rebasing in Magit 175 upvotes, 120 comments
  21. Agents that run while I sleep 175 upvotes, 126 comments
  22. Billion-Parameter Theories 84 upvotes, 57 comments
  23. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 257 upvotes, 203 comments
  24. RISC-V Is Sloooow 136 upvotes, 113 comments
  25. After outages, Amazon to make senior engineers sign off on AI-assisted changes 355 upvotes, 341 comments
  26. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 359 upvotes, 363 comments
  27. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 273 upvotes, 298 comments
  28. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 48 upvotes, 46 comments
  29. Show HN: What's my JND? – a colour guessing game 24 upvotes, 23 comments
  30. I built a programming language using Claude Code 99 upvotes, 145 comments

Last generated 7:30am, 11 March 2026. Source here.
